title: Bridge Paper: Interfacing behavioral and neural circuit models for habit formation.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.
**Phase context:** habit, automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, chunked behavior

Paper: Interfacing behavioral and neural circuit models for habit formation.
DOI: 10.1002/jnr.24581
Abstract: Habits are an important mechanism by which organisms can automate the control of behavior to alleviate cognitive demand. However, transitions to habitual control are risky because they lead to inflexible responding in the face of change. The question of how the brain controls transitions into habit is thus an intriguing one. How do we regulate when our repeated actions become automated? When is it advantageous or disadvantageous to release actions from cognitive control? Decades of research have
